blocks.content: She opened the door before he could knock.
The dress was dark blue. Her hair was up. She had a small thing pinned into it that caught the porch light.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Hey.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Hi.
He looked at her. She let him.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You look —

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Don't make it weird.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I was going to say you look really good, Daisy.
She smiled at the porch floor — the private one.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You too.
Her mom appeared behind her with a camera.

blocks.speaker: Daisy's Mom
blocks.content: Kit. Get in here. I want pictures.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yes, ma'am.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Mom —

blocks.speaker: Daisy's Mom
blocks.content: You are not leaving this house without pictures. Stand together.
They stood on the porch with the azalea bush behind them. Daisy's mom took six photos, at least. Daisy kept her eyes level and composed. Kit kept glancing at her.

blocks.speaker: Daisy's Mom
blocks.content: Kit, look at the camera.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Kit.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Sorry.
He looked at the camera.

Eli's mom drove them. The five of them piled into the minivan — the same configuration as homecoming, Eli in front, Marcus and Savannah in back, Kit and Daisy in the middle row. But this time there was no gap.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: For the record, I look incredible tonight.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: You look exactly like you always look.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Which is incredible. Thank you, Savannah.
In the middle row, Daisy's shoulder was against Kit's. Neither of them moved away.
